items.title: The Quest of the Silver Fleece, by W. E. B. Du Bois
items.content: <article>In the post-Reconstruction era, a young Black man and woman from the deep South struggle to overcome the economic and political fleecing of their community.</article>

items.title: Jesus the Son of Man, by Kahlil Gibran
items.content: <article>The life of Jesus of Nazareth is examined through the lens of the people around him.</article>

items.title: The Book of Khalid, by Ameen Rihani
items.content: <article>A Lebanese iconoclast emigrates to America and embarks on a quixotic quest for the truth.</article>

items.title: Short Fiction, by Harry Harrison
items.content: <article>A collection of science fiction stories by Harry Harrison, ordered by date of publication.</article>

items.title: Analects, by Confucius
items.content: <article>The paradigmatic Chinese sage expounds on leading an ethical and fulfilled life.</article>

items.title: June Moon, by Ring Lardner and George S. Kaufman
items.content: <article>A lyricist moves to New York City aspiring to make it big on Tin Pan Alley.</article>

items.title: The Condition of the Working Class in England, by Friedrich Engels
items.content: <article>A philosopher reports on the state of the proletariat in Victorian England.</article>

items.title: The Professor’s House, by Willa Cather
items.content: <article>As a middle-age professor moves house, he contemplates the legacy of his most brilliant student.</article>

items.title: Fred Gross Stories, by Ring Lardner
items.content: <article>A police detective attempts to live the American dream in the Chicago suburbs.</article>
